heroText: Vientiane is a capital city in Laos, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Vientiane
highlights.text: Vientiane is a capital city in Laos, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.label: 6th century
history.text: Other romanisations include "Viangchan" and "Wiangchan". Dvaravati city state kingdoms By the 6th century in the Chao Phraya River Valley, Mon peoples had coalesced to create the Dvaravati kingdoms.

history.label: 8th century
history.text: By the 8th century the Mon had pushed north to create city states, in Fa Daet (what later is Kalasin, northeastern Thailand), Sri Gotapura (Sikhottabong) near Tha Khek, Laos, Muang Sua (Luang Prabang), and.
The inscription says that in 1135, Văn Đan (Vientiane), a vassal of Zhenla (Khmer Empire), invaded Nghe An, and was repelled by the Duke; the Duke led an army chased the invaders as far.
He then appointed his middle son, also named Chueang, as ruler of Muang Phuan, and his youngest son, Lao Pao, as ruler of Vientiane. Lan Xang, and French colony In 1354, when Fa Ngum.
King Setthathirath officially established it as the capital of Lan Xang in 1563, to avoid Burmese invasion.

On 9 March 1946, French paratroopers arrived and reoccupied the city on 24 April 1946. Independence After the Laotian Civil War broke out between the Royal Lao Government and the Pathet Lao, in August.
In 1975, Pathet Lao troops moved towards the city and Americans began evacuating the capital.

history.label: Modern city
history.text: In Lao, viang (ວຽງ) refers to a 'walled city' whereas chan (ຈັນ, previously ຈັນທນ໌) derives from Sanskrit candana (चन्दन, /t͡ɕand̪ana/), 'sandalwood' and can be translated as the 'walled city of sandalwood'.

history.label: Today
history.text: Some believe it refers to the 'walled city of the moon' as chan can represent 'moon', and this was previously distinguished in writing as "ຈັນທຣ໌".

history.label: Regional role
history.text: In the north, Haripunjaya (Lamphun) emerged as a rival power to the Dvaravati.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The city states were “loosely bound politically, but were culturally similar” and introduced Therevada Buddhism from Sri Lankan missionaries throughout the region. Transition A reference to the name Vientiane can be seen on a.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: After successfully expelling the invaders, the younger Chueang marched eastward, annexed polities, and eventually captured Muang Phuan.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: During World War II, Vientiane fell and was occupied by Japanese forces.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: In December, Phoumi Nosavan then seized the capital, overthrew the Phouma Government, and installed Boun Oum as prime minister.
